Title: On the Vulgar Ambition of the Giant Gourd

Once a pumpkin knew its station: a modest lantern, a pie, a seasonal ornament content to sit
upon a stoop. Now we are asked to applaud a bloated 2,300-pound monster hauled to the fairground
on a flatbed, as though sheer size were an achievement of character. I, for one, decline to
genuflect before produce. Let the record show that restraint, too, is a virtue — even in a
vegetable.

Subject context:
ARTICLE 1: Town's giant pumpkin smashes the state record. A local grower's 2,300-pound pumpkin
took the state fair title after a season of careful tending.
